TRIAL IS JANUARY 3, 2012 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 220. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS DECEMBER 21, 2011. PLAINTIFFS TO SERVE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 14, 2011. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 ON DECEMBER 21, 2011 AT 1:30PM. NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 7, 2011 PLAINTIFFS TO PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS AND WHAT THAT KNOWLEDGE IS. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THE STATEMENT WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON A SUMMARY MOTION ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO STANDARD INTERROGATORIES BY THE END OF THE WEEK. DISCOVERY CUT-OFF ON THE DAY OF TRIAL, ABSENT STIPULATION BY THE PARTIES. DISCLOSURE OF EXPERTS 50 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL WITH PARED DOWN LIST 30 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL. REPORT OF ECONOMIC DAMAGE BY 11/30/11. ALL DOCUMENTS IN A FILED BANKRUPTCY TRUST CASE NEED TO BE PRODUCED BY 10/14/11 AND ANYTHING FILED AFTER 10/14/11 NEEDS TO BE PROVIDED WITHIN 3 DAYS OF THE FILING. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO ANY OTHER ISSUES THAT ANY OF THEM WISH TO HAVE ADDRESSED IN THE PREFERENCE ORDER. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OCTOBER 17, 2011 AT 2:00 P.M. RULING:
DEFENDANT GROENIGER & COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Groeniger & Co.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' complaint is off calendar as moot due to the tentative ruling on the demurrer. As guidance for the parties in the future, in the event that plaintiffs file an amended complaint meeting the requirements of Bockrath and re-alleging their allegations of punitive damages and agency, the court is likely to find that the punitive damages and agency allegations are sufficient to withstand a motion to strike. RULING:
DEFENDANT GROENIGER & COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Groeniger & Company's motion to strike is continued to September 7 to allow defendant time to file a reply. Plaintiffs filed a late opposition that included an attorney declaration demonstrating good cause for the court to consider the opposition. The court continues the hearing to give defendant time to reply. See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019). Defendant's reply, if any, must be filed and served by August 30. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison #4609 = (220/TLJ)
